articles.title: Pressure
articles.author: Mary Gustafson
articles.complete_text:
Little Miss Gray was like her name,
She never saw the sun,
For there was always work to do,
And errands to be run.

She had no time to stop before
The park where dahlias grew,
Or listen to a thrush call,
For there were things to do.

Her feet were just a pace behind
Her body as she went,
Head forward and against the wind,
Toil-turned and pressure-bent.

Had she but paused to look a moment
At the pink and buttercup,
She could have had a bit of resting
And a chance for looking up!
articles.notes: Complete poem with clear title and author attribution

articles.title: Song of Loneliness
articles.author: Vesta N. Lukei
articles.complete_text:
Oh, this I never knew,
Or noticed till tonight,
That distant trains create
A melody of flight,

And far across the hills
The unseen train tracks sing,
Attuned to loneliness—
Awake and listening.
articles.notes: Complete poem with clear title and author attribution

articles.title: Heart at Rest
articles.author: Beatrice K. Ekman
articles.complete_text:
The dark before the dawn
Engulfs the air;
A slumbering stillness waits
Upon the stair.

Quiet the fruited orchard
And the street;
No twittering of birds,
No hurrying feet.

Your dear, dark head
Upon the pillow pressed—
The house is full again,
My heart at rest.
articles.notes: Complete poem with clear title and author attribution

articles.title: Unexpressed
articles.author: Hilda V. Cameron
articles.complete_text:
Sometimes things happen,
Things we cannot express—
A sudden light,
A sense of joy,
An unforgettable caress.

There is no strange oblivion,
There is no curtain drawn
So close but we
May catch a glimpse,
Or feel the nearness
Of a loved one—gone.
articles.notes: Complete poem with clear title and author attribution

articles.title: Love Must Be Free
articles.author: Ada Marie Patten
articles.complete_text:
"I love this bird," my small son said,
"I'm surely glad I found it."
But when he looked, the bird was dead,
His hand too tight around it.

Some big folks, even, fail to see
In order to love rightly,
That which we love, we must set free—
Love dies if held too tightly.
articles.notes: Complete poem with clear title and author attribution

articles.title: A Little While
articles.author: Evelyn Fjeldsted
articles.complete_text:
Life is such a little while—
Remembered summers come and gone.
The winter's discontent removed,
The coming of a youth's new dawn.

Life is just a little while—
A great love safe within the past,
A love that was not realized,
But was understood at last.

Life's longest road is but a span—
A song, a sorrow to outlive,
A prelude's length of measured time,
With much to have and much to give.
articles.notes: Complete poem with clear title and author attribution
